name: WLF_GRW_GUIDE_ASSIGNMENTS
description: Table to store Assignment Records for Guide.

columns.Name: GUIDE_ID
columns.Comments: Guide Id for which assignment is created

columns.Name: GUIDE_TYPE
columns.Comments: Represents the type of guide Id

columns.Name: GUIDE_ASSIGNMENT_NUMBER
columns.Comments: User friendly uniquely identifying number for assignment

columns.Name: GUIDE_ASSIGNMENT_STATUS
columns.Comments: Status of the Guide assignment (e.g Active,InActive,Errored etc.)

columns.Name: STATUS_CHANGED_DATE
columns.Comments: Date to be captured during status change. For example during Withdrawal or Completion.

columns.Name: STATUS_CHANGE_COMMENT
columns.Comments: Comments to be captured during status change. For example during Withdrawal or Completion.

columns.Name: STATUS_CHANGE_TYPE
columns.Comments: To capture the change in status like Withdrawal or Completion.

columns.Name: ASSIGNED_ON_DATE
columns.Comments: The date that the assignment is assigned to the Guide user.

columns.Name: PERSON_ID
columns.Comments: Person Id for which Guide assignment record

columns.Name: WORK_ASSIGNMENT_ID
columns.Comments: Person work assignment id  for which Guide assignment record is.

columns.Name: REASON_CODE
columns.Comments: Reason for assignment status change. Example: useful when errored (if Person work assignment is part of multiple) Guide assignment created

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.
